executiveSummary:
## Medicaid Program Executive Summary - January 2026

National Medicaid enrollment reached 75.3 million beneficiaries as of January 2026, with virtually all states experiencing year-over-year enrollment declines following the conclusion of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. The most significant decreases occurred in Michigan (-7.6%) and California and Illinois (both -6.2%), while North Carolina emerged as the only large state to report positive growth (+0.5%). Total program expenditures for fiscal year 2024 reached $215.9 billion nationally, with a federal contribution of $142.0 billion representing approximately 66% of total spending. Per-enrollee costs averaged $2,869 nationally but varied substantially across states, ranging from Florida's $2,031 to Pennsylvania's $4,377, reflecting differences in benefit packages, provider payment rates, and population health needs.

Texas maintained its position as the third-largest Medicaid program with 4.0 million enrollees, experiencing a moderate 3.1% enrollment decline that aligned closely with peer states like Pennsylvania. However, Texas demonstrates significant operational contrasts with comparable large states: its per-enrollee spending of $2,988 falls below the national average and well under states like New York ($3,431) and Ohio ($3,561), while its managed care penetration rate of 93.3% substantially exceeds the national average of 83.2%. Quality performance presents a key area for improvement, with Texas scoring 62 out of 100 points and ranking 30th nationally—trailing behind higher-performing large states and falling into the "moderate" tier. Recent federal regulatory activity indicates heightened oversight focus on provider enrollment moratoria and state-directed payment arrangements, while ongoing procurement activities in Texas and peer states suggest continued system modernization efforts across multiple operational domains.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, representing significant scale and influence in the national landscape. The state's enrollment declined 3.1% year-over-year, tracking closely with the national trend as states complete post-pandemic eligibility redeterminations. Compared to peer large states, Texas shows moderate enrollment contraction—less severe than California (-6.2%) and Illinois (-6.2%), but similar to Pennsylvania (-3.1%). Among southern non-expansion states, Texas's decline is comparable to Florida (-4.0%) and Georgia (-3.6%), suggesting regional alignment in redetermination impacts. At $2,988 per enrollee, Texas spends 4.2% above the national average of $2,869, positioning it in the middle range among large states—well below high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and Ohio ($3,561), but above more restrictive programs like Florida ($2,031).

Texas demonstrates operational strengths in managed care implementation with 93.3% penetration, significantly exceeding the national average of 83.2% and reflecting the state's commitment to coordinated care delivery. However, quality outcomes remain a persistent challenge, with Texas scoring 62/100 on CMS quality measures—ranking 30th nationally and falling into the "moderate" performance tier. This places Texas below the national average of 65.6 and trails other large states that have achieved higher quality scores. The state's quality performance is particularly concerning when compared to southern peers like North Carolina, which has achieved both Medicaid expansion and higher quality outcomes. Key areas to monitor include Texas's ability to leverage its robust managed care infrastructure to drive quality improvements, potential federal regulatory changes affecting state-directed payments, and whether enrollment stabilization post-redetermination creates opportunities for enhanced program performance and outcomes measurement.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,869, indicating potential cost efficiency issues.

alerts.title: Michigan Medicaid Enrollment Drops 7.6% Year-Over-Year
alerts.description: Michigan experienced a 7.6% decline in Medicaid enrollment, the steepest drop among major states and well above the 10% threshold for concern.

alerts.title: Florida Shows Low Per-Enrollee Spending at $2,031
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,031 is 29% below the national average, which may indicate underfunding or access issues.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Scores
alerts.description: Mississippi (48), Oklahoma (51), Alabama (52), South Carolina (53), and Arkansas (54) all scored below 55 on quality measures, well below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Spending with Strong Quality Performance
alerts.description: Massachusetts combines high per-enrollee spending ($4,201) with the nation's top quality score (81), suggesting effective use of resources.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid quality score at risk - ranks #30 with only 62/100
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 on quality measures, ranking 30th of 50 states in moderate tier. This is significantly below the national average of 65.6 and well below high performers at 75+.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance creates contract risk and limits growth opportunities. HHSC likely faces federal pressure to improve outcomes.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement initiatives to HHSC leadership immediately

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment decline of 3.1% signals potential budget pressure
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY to 4.03M enrollees, tracking near the national declining trend. This represents meaningful membership loss in our primary contract state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment declines typically drive budget pressures and program changes. HHSC may need to optimize operations or adjust service delivery models.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review enrollment forecasting models with HHSC

executiveInsights.title: CMS announces nationwide provider enrollment moratorium affecting Medicaid
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows CMS announced temporary nationwide moratorium on enrollment for Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs. This regulatory change affects provider network management.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment freezes impact network adequacy and access to care metrics that Texas must maintain. This could complicate HHSC's provider relations strategy.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag provider enrollment moratorium impacts to HHSC provider relations team

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs above national average at $2,988 vs $2,869
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,988 per enrollee compared to the national average of $2,869, indicating higher than average program costs. Total Texas expenditures are $12.0B.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs suggest opportunities for cost management initiatives and efficiency improvements that Accenture could support.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost optimization opportunities with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: New CMS state directed payments rule may impact Texas managed care model
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows new CMS rule on Medicaid managed care state directed payments and targeted practitioner programs. Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: With Texas's high managed care penetration, changes to state directed payment rules could significantly impact how HHSC structures provider payments and contracts.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review new state directed payment requirements with HHSC managed care team

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment Decline Stabilization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY, creating pressure for operational efficiency and cost management improvements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop predictive analytics solution to optimize resource allocation during enrollment transitions.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality Score Enhancement
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, presenting significant improvement potential.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality improvement program targeting top-tier performance metrics.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed Care Optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas leads with 93.3% managed care penetration, requiring sophisticated coordination and oversight capabilities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop advanced managed care analytics platform to maximize the high penetration advantage.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal Regulatory Compliance
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple new CMS regulations on state directed payments and enrollment moratoria create complex compliance requirements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory tracking and compliance advisory service for Texas HHSC.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG Oversight Risk
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight and work plan items present audit and compliance scrutiny risks for large state programs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Implement proactive audit readiness and compliance monitoring framework.

riskOpportunity.label: Procurement Pipeline Expansion
riskOpportunity.description: Active Texas HHSC procurement portal and ESBD opportunities indicate upcoming contract competitions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish systematic procurement intelligence and opportunity development process.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost Efficiency Pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs at $2,988 create pressure for value-based innovations while maintaining service levels.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive cost optimization strategy with measurable ROI targets.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive Market Positioning
riskOpportunity.description: Large Texas market size ($12B expenditure, 4M enrollees) attracts significant competitor attention and investment.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen differentiated value proposition and deepen client relationships through innovation partnerships.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid serves 4,032,864 enrollees as of January 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: 12-Mo Enrollment Delta
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas enrollment declined 3.1% year-over-year, slightly better than the national average decline.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee annually, above the national average of $2,869.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 managed care enrollees.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on quality measures, ranking 30th nationally in the moderate tier.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two Texas-specific procurement opportunities are currently active through HHSC and ESBD portals.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Ten federal regulatory and OIG items are currently active affecting Medicaid programs nationwide.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No upcoming milestones are specified in the current dataset.
